transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Nice to meet you both.
Where are you calling in from today?
I can guess where you're calling in from, Sarah.
New York City?
Well, that's interesting that you say that.
I actually do live in New York City, but I'm in Seattle today.
Wow, dang.
Well, you still got the skyscrapers.
I know.
It really does.
I can see why you would say that.
Yeah, and also saw your LinkedIn, so, you know.
Oh, okay.
So that was a good signal.
That's part of it.
How about you, Rob?
I haven't checked your LinkedIn yet.
Where are you from?
No, I actually also live in Seattle, and I'll be in the office with Sarah in a little bit, but I do the morning routine at home, getting the kids ready for school.
So I'm at home, but in Seattle.
That's awesome.
I'm in Houston, Texas myself, and love Seattle.
I got a friend who lives over by, like, Green Lake, and he took me around Ballard, and we went to Olympic National Park, so I think we did
You're right.
That's awesome.
Rob, how come you haven't taken me to any of those places?
Thanks, Tyler.
Now I'm in trouble.
Well, to cover for Rob, those places are pretty far away.
So, you know.
You know what I've done, Tyler?
I've done a walk from Pioneer Square to here several times in the dark.
That's what I've done.
You just, yeah.
All right, Sarah, we'll get you out there.
Rob owes you a good dinner recommendation tonight.
That's what I'm thinking.
That's a good happy middle.
Yeah.
Well, it's a pleasure to meet.
I know that my CEO, Marcel, has had some conversations with David, and then we met with some of the other folks from the team.
But for context on my side, I'm the founding AE here, so I work hand-in-hand with Marcel.
And I guess we'd just love to know, you know, for today, is the goal really just to help introduce you to GrowthX and the way we work, like what you would get out of the partnership or...
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Is today's meeting a little different than that?
Just kind of help me catch up to speed.
Yeah, absolutely.
I just wanted to set a few minutes to introduce myself.
It was Yom Kippur when you guys met, so I was out of the office.
But I will probably be managing the relationship from a content oversight perspective and a day-to-day contact.
So I wanted just to frankly say hello.
And Rob is here because for the technical implementation, we just want to make sure that that goes as seamlessly as possible.
So similarly, I wanted to make sure that you had met him.
I was able to watch the meeting and saw the summary from last Thursday.
So I'm pretty much up to speed on the platform.
I don't know necessarily either that Rob needs to get the full review.
There's a deck and it's pretty straightforward.
And I kind of explained to Rob about how there would be a few different ways for the content to live on our site, whether that's like in a standalone section or on our resources page.
Which I do know that there will be.
A bevy of new content that happens pretty quickly.

My preference, I think, is that we have filters applied to our current resources page so that, like, it's a one-stop shop, although that's just rooted in, like, my editorial expertise and trying to keep things together.
If there's a better technical way to drive the goals of the content, then, of course, like, we'll follow your lead.
But Rob would be the one or his team, actually, to help implement that.

So he's just kind of looped in as a way for us to just kick off this project and to make sure that we're all aware that it's happening and you have a face to the names of the people that are going to be, you know, moving things forward.
Rob has a big team.
I have no team.
So you might not see Rob ever again, but he'll be behind the scenes.
And you or whoever I work with after you hand off the account will work directly with me.
And that was mainly, like, the point of the conversation.
I think I scheduled just 15 minutes just so I could just, like.
I have a moment to say hello, but if there's anything that needs to happen, we can talk about that now.
know David is revaluating the statement of work.
That's in his court right now.
You mentioned that if we could sign this week, we could even move up the date.
I think we're super eager to go as quickly as possible, so of course that's appealing.

David's traveling this week, so it might mean that he is a little bit slower to get to that statement of work, but I know that he's also really excited about this opportunity, so I imagine, I think he's flying back tomorrow.
I'll be sure to remind him, and hopefully we can move forward pretty quickly.
Yeah, that's perfect.
No, thanks for all that context, and if there's any questions that are top of mind for you, Sarah, happy to answer those here.

I will say, Rob, in terms of implementation, I think it'll be pretty easy because our preference is to just stage and publish in the CMS for you, and so if our team can get the access to be able to do that, and of course, Sarah, I liked it.
Anything that we draft, your team has eyes on and gives approval before we're going to go in stage and publish.
But most of our clients just prefer like that white glove end-to-end service.
And so I guess the only thing that was different that I heard from you was maybe trying to set up sections of the blog that don't exist today in tandem with the work that we'll do.
And that's where you see Rob being involved.
Exactly.
We, I've been at Reed for about 10 months.
We had no content, no resources page when I started.
We've since built it up.

And I think we have, I don't know, probably less than 20 articles, but we have something, case studies and other insights and landing pages for downloadable assets and other things that have been built over that time period.
And the, I'm excited to see that page like grow and expand exponentially.
I don't know if you looked at my LinkedIn page, maybe you know that I have an editorial background a long time ago.
So like all of this stuff really gets me jacked.
jacked.
And yeah, I would just be like, eventually I always wanted to grow what was present there and have a way for people to utilize that page in a more intuitive way.
think right now I think it's just chronological.
You all in our CMS is great.
The only consideration that comes top of mind to me right now, Rob, is like that Webflow access is like one seat only.
And I'm routinely kicked out.
It helps that I'm in New York because I try to do any Webflow like updates in the morning before Seattle gets up.
But if we have people that are working in the CMS frequently, then we might want to re-establish.
We would change that for this.
That would absolutely be the first thing we do.
Yep.
Okay, cool.
And then, yes, I do think that we should add filters.
And yeah, I mean, that's all I have.
And we, just to make sure we're on the same page here, we wouldn't be doing like web development as part of the work.

We would be focused on SEO, GEO strategy, generating the roadmap of all the assignments and creating all the articles and then doing the last mile execution in addition to like the LLM visibility, monitoring, analytics.
And so it's like with us, you get like an SEO agency, content creation agency, and also like Profound, like that platform, you know, the LLM visibility side.
It's all in one, but we would lean on Rob for like setting up those filters and we could definitely give, I think it'd be a collaboration in reality, Sarah, like we'd have topics, right?
That we would create content around and maybe those topics wouldn't be the filters themselves, right?
But they would be connected to, I don't know, like four or five different filters for the blog and you could help us align on what makes the most sense.
Yeah, and I'll be the liaison between the tech.
team on our side and your team and can help like move things along accordingly as well.
That's awesome.
Well, I can go into detail on anything growthx and show examples.
I guess help point me in the right direction since you already watched the call and have a good amount of contacts.
I I don't, I don't think I need that.
I guess my, my next question is like, once we sign the statement of work and we're ready to go, how can we be best prepared to move forward quickly?
Like what would be useful for me to prepare now so that we're ready to move forward once that's complete?
A hundred percent.
I would say that the team is looking for resources to better under, it's very simple, right?
To better understand like your ICP, your solution and your, your brand voice.
And so if there's any brand documents or guidelines, if there is work that the team has done around messaging or the ICP, if there's like a great sales deck or transcript or two, um, it's just.
Perfect.

Providing those resources ahead of the kickoff call and then thinking about who are maybe the two or three experts internally that we might want to have a one-on-one interview with the GrowthX team that could be like 30 minutes right after that kickoff call.
So since we're ahead of the curve here and the kickoff would be around a month from now, you've got plenty of time and those are like the two areas that we prep ahead of the kickoff.
Okay, great.
And then assuming they'll come back with all of your research and competitive analysis, tell us a little bit more about the strategic buckets.
We can work together to identify the filters.
Rob will do whatever, like sharing of updating to the team that like this is coming and to think about how they might want to structure it.
Perhaps updating our RevFlow contract in advance to make sure that this happens.
How quickly before, like between the November 3rd, assuming we get that date done.
So how quickly do you imagine that there's like content generated and we're posting it?
How long is that sprint?
Yeah, great question.
We are typically publishing the first articles halfway through the strategy sprint.
They're like calibration articles.
So the intention is like to align around quality.
But if we're moving fast, like we'll meet you at your pace, basically.
So, you know, we've like published 10 articles before the end of the strategy sprint that were on the blog, right?
We've in some cases like gone up to 20.

If we've got alignment around the roadmap and if we can get to the quality quickly and it's not like a super cumbersome review process, I anticipate like, yeah, 10 to 20 is what you could expect in phase one.
And what's great about this too is we give teams the ability to opt out by the last week of phase one in terms of the phase two growth execution.
And so that opt out date, it's at a time.
We're like, you fully understand what you would be getting out of phase two, the quality of the articles, the roadmap of assignments that we'd work on, the way that our team would collaborate.

So, you know, that, I would say, like, if there's a couple areas that can help us secure signature by Friday, one is, you know, David asked about, he reached out to Marcel directly on this, but he asked about, like, do we have language for opt-out at a certain milestone?
And he was asking for, like, a one-time-out at month six.
I think that would be pretty tough to get, like, approved because how long we give to opt-out in phase one.
And so I think, you know, if we can get David okay with the seven weeks that the team can opt-out, that would be helpful.
We can potentially be a little flexible on pricing, too, since, like, we're both Madrona companies and, you know, the team is going to be great collaborators.
Like, we feel...
really confident about that, but we would need to just have the opt-out be during the first seven weeks, and then we can maybe reduce price a little bit to help out.
The other thing he mentioned was limitation of liability.
No problem there.
We'll put liability language.
That should satisfy his concern with that point.
And then the last point, and this one's a little tricky, it was like, I want to see if we can work with you to migrate off Fathom to read.ai.
I know the legacy data was important to migrate, and we've got a few things in the works that would simplify that flow.
We, that one's tough just because it would need to like run through our IT person, you know, like our head of IT would sort of do the evaluation and make the call there.
And so I don't think we're going to be able to commit to buying read.ai since we weren't like actively evaluating other software.
But what we can do is we can set up, you know, a discovery call and...
Have our IT person take a sincere, serious look, if that helps.
Thank you for sharing all that.
I love that.
Maybe I wasn't supposed to share it.
Maybe I wasn't supposed to no, no.
But that could get in the way of signature.
Yeah.
There are so many things that I appreciate and love about David, and that is just one of them.
So it's nice to see it.
I can't comment.
And I think that you should respond directly to David on all of those questions.
I can reinforce how excited we are and that we connected and we have a plan in place to move forward.
But I think it would be best if Marcel or you get back to David on those particular aspects, if you don't mind.
That's perfect.
I'll go ahead and make sure that Marcel responds by tomorrow at the absolute latest.
And I think you at least have that context.
So if David asks you about any of that stuff, you can...
I'm going to make sure we have the talk track around the seven weeks and everything that we're going to do in those first seven weeks.
But hopefully we can almost triangulate to make sure that we reach alignment as fast as we can.
Because the kickoff date is going to be tough to secure as early if we can't see the light at the end of the tunnel, right?
Right, right.
Cool.
Well, I'm excited.
And I am always a phone call away.
So I'm happy to, you know, answer any questions that come up and definitely can share any resources or examples if you need them.
Okay, great.
Well, you know where to find me.
Thanks so much for taking the time this morning.
Rob, thank you as well.
And we'll be in touch if we have any further questions.
Looking forward to more.
Awesome.
Enjoy the Pacific Northwest.
Talk soon.
Thank you.
Talk to you later.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introductory sync between GrowthX and Read AI to discuss partnership logistics and next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=93.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Sarah (Read AI) will manage content oversight and be the day-to-day contact; Rob (Read AI) will oversee technical implementation](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=125.0)
  - [GrowthX to handle SEO strategy, content creation, and LLM visibility; Read AI to manage CMS access and blog structure](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=410.0)
  - [Aiming for quick start: 10-20 articles possible in Phase 1 (7 weeks), with opt-out option at end](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=593.0)
  - [Contract negotiation ongoing: David (Read AI) reviewing SOW; GrowthX flexible on pricing, adding liability language](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=671.0)

## Topics

### Team Introductions and Roles

  - [Tyler: Founding AE at GrowthX, works closely with CEO Marcel](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=93.0)
  - [Sarah: Content oversight and day-to-day contact at Read AI (10 months tenure)](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=125.0)
  - [Rob: Technical implementation lead at Read AI](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=143.0)
  - [Sarah missed previous meeting due to Yom Kippur, but reviewed recording and summary](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=125.0)

### Content Implementation Strategy

  - [GrowthX to stage and publish in Read AI's CMS](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=283.0)
  - [Read AI team to review and approve before publishing](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=283.0)
  - Current resources page: \<20 articles, chronological order
  - [Plan to add filters and potentially restructure for intuitive navigation](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=328.0)
  - [GrowthX scope: SEO, content strategy, roadmap creation, article writing, LLM visibility monitoring](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=410.0)
  - [Read AI to handle web development aspects (e.g., adding filters)](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=410.0)

### Technical Considerations

  - [Current Webflow access limited to one seat, causing login issues](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=376.0)
  - [Rob to update RevFlow contract for expanded access](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=572.0)
  - [GrowthX team needs CMS access for content publishing](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=283.0)

### Partnership Kickoff Preparation

  - [Read AI to provide:](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=515.0)
    1.  ICP (Ideal Customer Profile) information
    2.  Solution details
    3.  Brand voice guidelines
    4.  Relevant sales decks/transcripts
  - [Identify 2-3 internal experts for 30-min interviews with GrowthX team](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
  - [Kickoff call expected \~1 month from now](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)

### Content Production Timeline

  - [First articles typically published midway through strategy sprint](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=607.0)
  - [Potential for 10-20 articles in Phase 1 (7 weeks), depending on alignment and review process](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=593.0)
  - [Opt-out option available at end of Phase 1 (7 weeks)](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=648.0)

### Contract Negotiation Points

  - [David (Read AI) inquired about:](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=671.0)
    1.  Opt-out clause (GrowthX prefers 7-week opt-out vs. 6-month)
    2.  Limitation of liability (GrowthX agreeable)
    3.  Migrating from Fathom to Read AI (GrowthX open to discovery call, but no commitment)
  - [GrowthX potentially flexible on pricing due to Madrona connection](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=710.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Marcel (GrowthX CEO) to respond to David's inquiries by tomorrow](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=808.0)
  - [Sarah to reinforce excitement and readiness to Read AI team](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=808.0)
  - [GrowthX to provide detailed 7-week plan and value proposition](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=840.0)
  - [Read AI to prepare ICP, brand, and solution documentation for kickoff](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=515.0)
  - [Rob to update RevFlow contract for expanded CMS access](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=572.0)
  - [Await David's review of Statement of Work (SOW)](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=246.0)
  - [Schedule discovery call between Read AI and GrowthX IT for potential migration from Fathom](https://fathom.video/share/vzNZ27psT9KnGVSHxpawoKeHHRVq1yLz?tab=summary&timestamp=741.0)

action_items.description: Remind David to review SOW upon return from travel tmrw
Update Webflow contract to allow multiple user access for GrowthX team
Plan implementation of filters on Read.ai resources page for new GrowthX content
Compile resources on Read.ai ICP, solution, brand voice. Prep sales deck/transcripts, brand guidelines, messaging docs for GrowthX team
Identify 2-3 internal experts for 30-min interviews w/ GrowthX team post-kickoff
Ensure Marcel responds to David's contract Qs (opt-out, liability, Read.ai migration) by tmrw latest
